title: Acute Pelvic Pain - Ruptured Ovarian Cyst
chief_complaint: 22-year-old with sudden onset right lower quadrant pain mid-cycle. Pain sharp, now improving. No fever. No vaginal bleeding. Regular menstrual cycles.

physical_exam.abdomen: Mild RLQ tenderness, no rebound, no guarding
physical_exam.pelvic: Right adnexal mild tenderness, no masses

hidden_data.us_pelvis: Free fluid in pelvis, collapsed 4cm cyst right ovary with internal debris, normal left ovary

key_learning_points: Mid-cycle pain + free fluid = ruptured cyst
Surgery only if hemodynamically unstable or torsion suspected

patient_responses.pain: Sudden sharp pain that's getting better now

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Ruptured ovarian cyst - self-limited.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a ruptured cyst. The free fluid + collapsed cyst confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Ruptured cyst: Supportive care with NSAIDs. Usually resolves in 24-48h. Follow-up US in 6 weeks to confirm resolution. Surgery only if hemodynamically unstable.
